ddls:
CREATE TABLE "manufacturing_steps" (
  "step_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manufacturing step */,
  "tl_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the production line or task list */,
  "start_at_step" INTEGER /* The step number where the manufacturing process starts */,
  "stage" INTEGER /* Current stage of the manufacturing process */,
  "stage_time" INTEGER /* Time taken for the current stage in seconds */,
  "time_beyond_queue_clearance" INTEGER /* Time spent beyond queue clearance in seconds */,
  "residual_queue" INTEGER /* Current residual queue length indicating potential bottlenecks */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  "step_description" TEXT /* Description of the manufacturing step */,
  "expected_duration" INTEGER /* Expected duration for the manufacturing step in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("step_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_manufacturing_steps_tl_id FOREIGN KEY ("tl_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_lines" (
  "line_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production line */,
  "line_name" TEXT /* Name of the production line */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the production line */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the production line in the facility */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum capacity of the production line */,
  "equipment_list" TEXT /* List of equipment used on the production line */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tasks" (
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the production line the task belongs to */,
  "task_name" TEXT /* Name of the task */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the task */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the task */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the task */,
  "task_status" TEXT /* Status of the task (e.g., pending, in progress, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., operator, supervisor, manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the task associated with the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., queue delay, machine failure) */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert occurred */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the alert */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert resolution (e.g., pending, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "queue_management" (
  "queue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each queue record */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the production line */,
  "current_length" INTEGER /* Current length of the queue */,
  "average_wait_time" REAL /* Average wait time in seconds */,
  "max_wait_time" REAL /* Maximum wait time recorded in seconds */,
  "queue_status" TEXT /* Status of the queue (e.g., normal, congested) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("queue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_queue_management_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_status" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the production line the machine is assigned to */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the machine (e.g., operational, under maintenance) */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance performed */,
  "next_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "machine_type" TEXT /* Type of machine */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the machine */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machine_status_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shift_schedules" (
  "shift_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shift */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user assigned to the shift */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the production line for the shift */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the shift */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the shift */,
  "shift_type" TEXT /* Type of shift (e.g., morning, evening, night) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shift_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shift_schedules_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shift_schedules_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "process_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "step_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manufacturing step associated with the log */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action occurred */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the action */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_process_logs_step_id FOREIGN KEY ("step_id") REFERENCES manufacturing_steps ("step_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the production line */,
  "production_output" INTEGER /* Total production output (number of items produced) */,
  "downtime" INTEGER /* Total downtime recorded in seconds */,
  "efficiency" REAL /* Efficiency calculated as a percentage */,
  "quality_rating" REAL /* Quality rating of the production line (e.g., 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the machine that underwent maintenance */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of maintenance performed */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., routine, emergency) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the maintenance performed */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machine_status ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training_records" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who attended the training */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date of the training session */,
  "training_type" TEXT /* Type of training (e.g., safety, operational procedures) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of training in minutes */,
  "trainer_name" TEXT /* Name of the trainer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_records_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user providing feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when feedback was submitted */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or suggestions provided by the user */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (e.g., 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
